name: tail_logs
description: Streams or fetches recent logs from a deployed application or a running service. This typically involves using a platform-specific CLI command (e.g., 'flyctl logs', 'vercel logs <deploymentId>') or querying a logging service.

project_path.description: Optional. The root directory path of the project, if needed by the logging CLI (e.g., for Vercel project linking).

service_identifier.description: Identifier for the service whose logs are being requested (e.g., Fly.io app name, Vercel deployment ID or project ID, Docker container ID).

log_source_type.description: Specifies the platform or type of log source to use the correct underlying command or API.

lines.description: Number of recent log lines to fetch. Applicable if not continuously following/streaming. For streaming, this might be the initial number of lines to show.

follow.description: If true, attempts to stream logs continuously (like 'tail -f'). If false, fetches a batch of recent logs. Note: Continuous streaming might have implications for how the orchestrator handles the tool call (e.g., long-lived connection or periodic polling).

raw_cli_command.description: Optional. If log_source_type is 'generic_cli', this is the exact command to run to fetch/tail logs.

hints.idempotent: true for fetching recent logs (follow=false), false for streaming (follow=true as it's a continuous process)
hints.description: Fetches or streams logs. Non-destructive. Can be long-running if follow=true.
